# mhueni.github.io

Personal website of Matthias Hüni, Software Engineer & Maker from Zurich, Switzerland + "Wo go bade in Züri?" pool crowd monitor.

## Key files

- [index.html](index.html) — Landing page
- [wo-go-bade.html](wo-go-bade.html) — Pool crowd monitor app
- [assets/main.js](assets/main.js) — Typing animation
- [assets/main.css](assets/main.css) — Landing page styles
- [util/bathdata.php](util/bathdata.php) — PHP proxy for city bath data XML

## Tech stack

- Static HTML/CSS/JS frontend
- PHP backend (single proxy script)
- WebSocket for real-time crowd data
- Web app manifest + SVG favicon

## Pool crowd monitor

### Data sources
- WebSocket `wss://badi-public.crowdmonitor.ch:9591/api` — crowd counts per pool UID
- Stadt Zürich Open Data Webservice (proxied via PHP) — water temperature + official open/closed; data: https://data.stadt-zuerich.ch/dataset/wassertemperaturen-freibaeder/resource/548d1ceb-1daf-4cf9-a14a-92c86326824d
- Static POOL_HOURS — hardcoded opening periods per UID

### WHITELIST mapping
- Object mapping crowd-monitor UID → city POI ID (e.g. `'SSD-10': 'seb6945'`)
- Card background: green if "offen", red if "geschlossen"
- Label format when open with city data: `"offen (Wasser {temp}°C, HH:MM Uhr)"`
- City data only fetched on localhost/127.0.0.1/mhueni.ch/*.mhueni.ch

### Render flow
1. fetchCityData() → JSON from PHP proxy → Map<poiid, data>
2. WebSocket message → filter by WHITELIST → attach city data
3. renderPools() → sort by favorites then name → compute label/color → render cards
4. 60s interval → re-evaluate isOpen(), update label + color

## index.html conventions
- `<h2>` for section headings, `<p class="subtitle">` for descriptions
- `.project-list` `<ul>` for project listings
- Links open in new tab (`target="_blank"`)
- Generator meta: `vibe coded with opencode`

## main.js
- Typing animation for `.subtitle` elements
- SPEED = 15 ms per character
- IntersectionObserver: starts typing when scrolled into view
